title: Client Onboarding Automation for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Done-for-you client onboarding for web design agencies. New client setup, kickoff packs, and asset collection that run on schedule, in your voice.
excerpt: A done-for-you onboarding sequence for web design agencies. Every new client gets a clean kickoff, signed paperwork, and assets collected without you chasing.

sections.intro:
You won the project. Now the slow part starts. The contract has to go out, the deposit invoice has to follow, the kickoff call has to be booked, and you still need logos, copy, brand assets, hosting access, and answers to a long questionnaire. Most web design agencies lose the first week of every engagement to this chase, and clients form their first opinion of you during exactly that week.

The Ayothedoc AI Operating System installs a done-for-you onboarding sequence that runs the moment a proposal is accepted. It sends the agreement, triggers the deposit invoice, books the kickoff, opens the project workspace, and collects assets in your voice and on your schedule. You stay the designer. The system handles the setup.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Capabilities layer of the AIOS. Capabilities are the done-for-you workflows that draft, route, collect, and follow up so the owner does not have to. Onboarding is one of the highest leverage capabilities for a design agency because it touches every new engagement.

  • Context: the system knows your packages, your tone, your standard questionnaire, and your kickoff checklist.
  • Connections: it works from your existing CRM, inbox, calendar, billing, and document tools.
  • Capabilities: the onboarding sequence itself, including reminders and asset chasing.
  • Cadence: it runs on a schedule, so reminders go out at the right times without you queuing them.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
• A proposal moves to accepted, and within 5 minutes the client receives the agreement and the deposit invoice in your voice.
  • Once the deposit lands, the kickoff call is offered with your live availability and confirmed automatically.
  • A branded welcome email goes out with the project workspace link, the asset checklist, and the questionnaire.
  • Assets and answers are collected in one place, with structured reminders on day 2, day 5, and day 8 if items are missing.
  • You get a single daily summary of which clients are stuck, what is missing, and what needs a human reply.
  • SLA: from accepted proposal to kickoff-ready client, under 10 business days in the standard case, with no manual chasing from you.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes): we walk through your current onboarding, your packages, your tools, and where clients currently go quiet. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days): we build the sequence end to end, write the templates in your voice, wire it into your existing CRM, inbox, calendar, and billing, and run it against two recent clients as a dry run before go-live.
  3. Operate (ongoing): the system runs every new engagement, and we ship one new automation per week, tuning the onboarding sequence as your packages and team change. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time from signed proposal to first asset received drops from 7 to 14 days down to 1 to 3 days in most agencies.
  • You recover roughly 3 to 6 hours per new client on admin, scheduling, and chasing.
  • Deposit collection speeds up by 2 to 5 days because the invoice goes out the same hour the proposal is accepted.
  • Fewer projects stall in week one, which protects your launch dates and your cash flow.
  • Standing guarantee: across the full AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month, or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Will the emails sound like us, or like a generic agency?

They sound like you. During the audit and install we capture your voice from existing client emails, proposals, and kickoff notes, then the system drafts from that. You approve the templates before anything goes live.

Do we have to change our project management or billing tools?

No. The AIOS is tool-agnostic and connects to what you already use for CRM, billing, calendar, docs, and project management. If something is genuinely holding you back, we will say so, but the default is to work with your stack.

What happens when a client goes silent on assets?

The system sends structured reminders on a cadence you approve, escalates to a short personal nudge in your voice, and surfaces stuck clients in your daily summary so you can step in once, not five times.
